summary: Mitchell Hashimoto advocates for using Nix alongside traditional Dockerfiles to solve the persistent problem of inconsistent environments across development, CI, and production. The approach uses Nix as a single source of truth for building software, then leverages multi-stage Docker builds to create minimal final images. While Dockerfiles alone are simple, they create duplicated effort and brittle configurations when combined with separate local dev and CI setups. The key insight is that Nix's benefits compound when the same configuration powers all environments, eliminating 'works on my machine' issues entirely.
key_points: Traditional setups require maintaining three separate environment configurations (local dev, CI, production), which inevitably drift and break
Nix serves as a single source of truth that works consistently across Linux, Mac, CI, and Docker with zero duplication
The Dockerfile approach uses multi-stage builds: Nix builds in a builder container, then only the closure of dependencies gets copied to a scratch-based final image
The Nix store closure represents the mathematically smallest possible set of files needed to run your application
Learning Nix has upfront costs but eliminates entire categories of environment-related problems permanently
money_quotes: I'm serious, I haven't had a 'works on my machine [but not on others]' issue in years. Not a single one.
It's like someone looking at a river and lamenting about having to wade through to cross it while I'm riding a bicycle across a bridge.
I'm actively trying to work through my Nix God complex.
In isolation, I submit to you that using Nix with Docker is mostly just harder than using Docker by itself.
These problems all go away with Nix. Nix is your single source of truth of what you need to build and run your software.
I think the real pain point is you want your environments to Just Work.
It does not depend on or conflict with your local system at all. If you have other versions of Python, Flask, etc. installed, it won't matter at all; your app is perfectly packaged.
key_insight: The compounding benefits of using Nix across all environments—not just Docker—transform it from being harder than plain Dockerfiles to being dramatically easier while eliminating entire categories of 'works on my machine' problems.
